subject: Geography
chapter: Planning and Sustainable Development in Indian Context

remember: Students will be able to recall the concept of planning and sustainable development in the Indian context.
Students will be able to identify the key features of the Five-Year Plans in India.
Students will be able to list the various policies and initiatives for sustainable development in India.
understand: Students will be able to understand the importance of planning in achieving sustainable development.
Students will be able to explain the role of the Planning Commission in India.
Students will be able to describe the concept of decentralization in planning.
apply: Students will be able to apply the principles of planning to a case study of a sustainable development project in India.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of a policy or initiative for sustainable development in India.
Students will be able to suggest measures for improving the planning process in India.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of globalization on planning and sustainable development in India.
Students will be able to compare the planning approaches of different states in India.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of civil society in planning and sustainable development in India.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT, students will be able to understand the concept of planning and sustainable development in the Indian context.
Students will be able to analyze the role of the government in promoting sustainable development in India.
Students will be able to evaluate the effectiveness of various policies and initiatives for sustainable development in India.
board_exam_objectives: To test the students' understanding of the concept of planning and sustainable development in the Indian context.
To evaluate the students' ability to analyze the impact of planning on sustainable development in India.
To assess the students' knowledge of the various policies and initiatives for sustainable development in India.
